kept.text:
Galaxy Digital Inc. ( $GLXY) tarafından açıklanan güçlü bilanço ve CoreWeave ( $CRWV) için kiralanan veri merkezinin planlanan zaman çizelgesine uygun şekilde ilerlediğinin teyit edilmesi sonrası analistler beklentilerini güncelledi.

Bu tarz gelişmeler, hikayenin sadece kağıt üzerinde değil, sahada da ilerlediğini gösteriyor.

Yatırım tavsiyesi değildir.

author.name: Yunis Rzayev
author.description: 🎓 Law graduate | 📊 Equity & Crypto Investor Focused on financial markets, regulation & behavioral finance. Legal perspective in a volatile world.

quoted_tweet.text:
$GLXY için söyleyeyim: bu hisse tarafında hem yönetim hem de iş modeli konusunda uzun süredir olumlu düşünüyordum ve sayfamda da bunu sıkça paylaştım.

Ama şimdilik piyasa aynı fikirde değil, fiyat hareketi bunu net gösteriyor.

Yine de uzun vadeli bakışım değişmiş değil. Bir gün piyasanın da bu hikayeyi farklı okumaya başlayacağını düşünüyorum.

Yatırım tavsiyesi değildir.

#GLXY #GalaxyDigital #CryptoStocks #BitcoinMining #DigitalAssets #StockMarket #Investing

author.name: CoreWeave

kept.text:
$CRWV CoreWeave price target raised to $150 from $140 at Oppenheimer    

Oppenheimer raised the firm's price target on CoreWeave to $150 from $140 and keeps an Outperform rating on the shares ahead of quarterly results. The firm expects revenue at the high end of guidance and for management to raise 2026 revenue materially above $12B -$13B. This is based on its industry checks that indicate CoreWeave is building out capacity faster than anticipated and can add another 1GW by the September quarter. Oppenheimer also expects the industry-wide shortage of AI data center capacity to persist for multiple years and anticipate that CoreWeave can grow to at least 6GW during that time.

kept.text:
12 hours later.

SoftBank -9%. Worst day since November.
CoreWeave -5.8%.
Oracle -4%.
Nvidia -1.6%.

Microsoft +1%. Apple +1.2%.

The market sorted AI today.

The companies who own AI.

The companies who rent OpenAI.

author.name: George Pu
author.description:
AI is eating everything you were told to build. 

It ate mine first. Now I build things it can't.

quoted_tweet.text:
We all thought OpenAI was flying.

Sam on stage everywhere.

Trillion-dollar valuation talk.

IPO by year-end.

A few annoying lawsuits, sure.

Some competition from Anthropic and Google, fine.

But still the winner. Still the kingmaker.

Then the curtain moved this week.

ChatGPT didn't hit 1 billion users like they promised.

They lost the coding market to Anthropic.

Their CFO is freaking out about $600B in compute bills.

The board is questioning Sam's spending.

The CFO doesn't even want to IPO this year.

Sam's pushing it anyway.

That 'freedom' deal with Microsoft yesterday?

That was Sam running to AWS, Google, and Oracle.

Because one Microsoft couldn't save him.

The whole time we were watching the show.

Nobody was watching the books.

author.name: George Pu
author.description:
AI is eating everything you were told to build. 

It ate mine first. Now I build things it can't.

tweet.text:

author.name: Mike Investing
author.description: • Stock/Option Trader 📈 • 24/7 profit 💎 • Founder of Mikes Trading 👑 • I’m not a financial advisor • (You are responsible for your choices)

tweet.text:
Today is the day!!!

$GOOGL

$MSFT

$AMZN

$META

all have earnings.

I'm super pumped and feel like all my favorite movies are coming out all at once haha

Who's excited? I probably won't be able to sleep...

It doesn't matter if you hold
$NBIS
$IREN

$CRWV

$MRVL

$AVGO

$NVDA

$AMD

... or pretty much any other AI or non-AI stock.

This is the day where the entire narrative for the next quarter gets confirmed or changed...

What are you guys looking for? Any special data points you care about?

For me it's obviously capex, cloud growth, and how they talk about enterprise AI adoption and ROI.

My dream case scenario would be this: all of them say a) we're seeing AI take off in the enterprise, b) this is because models are getting better, customers are getting more sophisticated in using them, and multiple high-ROI use cases are emerging, c) this is why we will double down on capex.

Bottom line should be: AI is unstoppable and this trend will continue... probably forever.

On the other hand, the bears obviously want to hear things like "AI cycle," lowering capex, basically just things that point towards disappointing ROI.

Because valuations are still so high across the entire market, I think we need very strong earnings to just stay flat. So basically if earnings are kinda neutral, meeting expectations, I wouldn't be surprised to see red across the board.

I'm a perma bull, but if valuations are this high, the odds are asymmetrically stacked against us bulls.

What's interesting though is to highlight that
$MSFT
and
$META
are actually quite cheap compared to a lot of very low-quality garbage in the market.

Both can print cash. Both have strong moats. Both have insanely valuable data. Both are destined to win long-term unless they seriously screw up execution for like 3-5 years in my view.
